title: Further Continuance of the Federal Emergency Management Agency Review Council
summary: Executive Order 14397, issued on March 24, 2026, further extends the Federal Emergency Management Agency Review Council. This council, initially established by Executive Order 14180 and previously continued by Executive Order 14378, will now remain in effect until 10 days after its required report is submitted to the President, or until May 29, 2026, whichever comes first. The order also designates the Secretary of Homeland Security to perform the President's functions concerning the council under the Federal Advisory Committee Act. This new order supersedes sections 1 and 2 of Executive Order 14378. General provisions clarify that the order does not affect existing departmental authorities or OMB functions and is subject to applicable law and appropriations. The Department of Homeland Security will cover the costs of publishing this order.
